description:
You are an emergency planner updating the Emergency Action Plan for a water treatment facility that stores Chlorine, Ammonia, and Hydrogen Cyanide. Using the CAMEO Chemicals website (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/), compile the 60-minute AEGL-1, AEGL-2, and AEGL-3 values in ppm for Chlorine, Ammonia (anhydrous), and Hydrogen Cyanide. Create a structured AEGL Exposure Threshold Report at /home/ga/Documents/aegl_report.txt. The report must include a header, the 60-minute AEGL values for each chemical, and a conclusion identifying which chemical has the lowest AEGL-2 value and therefore poses the greatest acute inhalation hazard. 

For context, the AEGL tiers represent:
- AEGL-1: Discomfort, non-disabling effects
- AEGL-2: Irreversible or serious, long-lasting health effects (used for evacuation planning)
- AEGL-3: Life-threatening or fatal effects
